betterquesting:10.desc:8:
And with the knowledge of Truth, comes the beraking of lies.
Lies that are in no small part responsible for many of the nefarious acts of those willing to trample entire people for their own sake, for their pointless claims to power. But also lies that we tell ourselves, patterns of thought that delude us into doing what is bad for us.
The Cyclop Realmshifters, midless killers fighing all who come in their proximity; forgetting their vows to fight a war they cannot fight. The lie that by fighting back, by refuting their culture of love and peace, they would be able to protect love and peace.
The Testificates, refusing to accept the signs that their god, their guardian, had fallen. The lie that hoping for a miracolous return would excuse their sloth, their unwillingness to act in theor own interests, instead dividing their people in septs and cults, each hiding away knowledge of the Arbiter from the others.
The fallen Wolves of Asgard, grasping at the symbols of their power. The lie that, by just staying alive, while regressing into nothing more than beasts, the legacy of their realm would endure with them.
Thepeople of the Twilight Forest, postponing their demise through study, and avoiding asking for help until the very last minute. The lie that, by refusing to ask for help, with enough effort, one can be their own savior.
Lies, and yet no liers. Just people struggling, and falling into misguided actions that made their struggles worse.
Recovery for these people will take a lot more than truths, and help from many sources, but truths are what I can gather for now. And from these truths I can learn, I can learn how to approach my own mission, my own call, my own struggle, which will inevitably come.
